# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use safe data-only formats and validate the resulting structure/types before use.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use safe data-only formats such as JSON and validate structure/types before use.
- Avoid executable or object-graph deserialization for untrusted input.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not deserialize untrusted data with unsafe native object deserializers.
- Do not execute or instantiate attacker-controlled object graphs.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cookie;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;

class UserPreferencesHandler
{
    /**
     * The cookie name for storing user preferences.
     */
    private const COOKIE_NAME = 'user_prefs';

    /**
     * Cookie lifetime in minutes (30 days).
     */
    private const COOKIE_LIFETIME = 43200;

    /**
     * Allowed preference keys and their expected types.
     */
    private const ALLOWED_KEYS = [
        'theme' => 'string',
        'language' => 'string',
        'timezone' => 'string',
        'font_size' => 'integer',
        'notifications_enabled' => 'boolean',
        'sidebar_collapsed' => 'boolean',
        'items_per_page' => 'integer',
        'date_format' => 'string',
        'currency' => 'string',
        'layout' => 'string',
    ];

    /**
     * Default preferences.
     */
    private const DEFAULTS = [
        'theme' => 'light',
        'language' => 'en',
        'timezone' => 'UTC',
        'font_size' => 14,
        'notifications_enabled' => true,
        'sidebar_collapsed' => false,
        'items_per_page' => 25,
        'date_format' => 'Y-m-d',
        'currency' => 'USD',
        'layout' => 'default',
    ];

    /**
     * Store user preferences in a cookie.
     *
     * Uses JSON encoding (a safe data-only format) instead of PHP serialize
     * to prevent object injection vulnerabilities.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return void
     */
    public function storePreferences(array $preferences): void
    {
        $validated = $this->validateAndSanitize($preferences);

        // Use JSON — a safe, data-only format — never PHP serialize for cookie data.
        $encoded = json_encode($validated, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR);

        Cookie::queue(
            self::COOKIE_NAME,
            $encoded,
            self::COOKIE_LIFETIME,
            '/',
            null,
            true,  // secure: only send over HTTPS
            true,  // httpOnly: not accessible via JavaScript
            false,
            'Lax'  // SameSite attribute
        );
    }

    /**
     * Restore user preferences from the cookie.
     *
     * Uses json_decode (a safe data-only format) instead of unserialize
     * to prevent PHP object injection attacks from untrusted cookie data.
     *
     * SECURITY NOTE: We NEVER use unserialize() on cookie data or any
     * untrusted input. unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) would allow
     * attackers to inject arbitrary PHP objects, leading to remote code
     * execution via POP chains.
     *
     * @return array
     */
    public function getPreferences(): array
    {
        $cookieValue = Cookie::get(self::COOKIE_NAME);

        if ($cookieValue === null) {
            return self::DEFAULTS;
        }

        // SAFE: Use json_decode — a data-only deserializer that cannot
        // instantiate objects or trigger magic methods.
        // NEVER use: unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) — this is vulnerable
        // to PHP Object Injection attacks.
        $decoded = json_decode($cookieValue, true);

        if (!is_array($decoded)) {
            Log::warning('UserPreferencesHandler: Invalid cookie data detected, returning defaults.', [
                'cookie_value_type' => gettype($decoded),
            ]);
            return self::DEFAULTS;
        }

        // Validate structure and types before use
        return $this->validateAndSanitize($decoded);
    }

    /**
     * Update a single preference.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $value
     * @return void
     * @throws \InvalidArgumentException
     */
    public function setPreference(string $key, mixed $value): void
    {
        if (!array_key_exists($key, self::ALLOWED_KEYS)) {
            throw new \InvalidArgumentException("Unknown preference key: {$key}");
        }

        $preferences = $this->getPreferences();
        $preferences[$key] = $value;

        $this->storePreferences($preferences);
    }

    /**
     * Get a single preference value.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $default
     * @return mixed
     */
    public function getPreference(string $key, mixed $default = null): mixed
    {
        $preferences = $this->getPreferences();

        return $preferences[$key] ?? $default ?? (self::DEFAULTS[$key] ?? null);
    }

    /**
     * Clear all stored preferences.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function clearPreferences(): void
    {
        Cookie::queue(Cookie::forget(self::COOKIE_NAME));
    }

    /**
     * Validate and sanitize preferences array.
     *
     * Only allows known keys with expected types. This ensures that even if
     * an attacker tampers with the cookie, only safe scalar values in a
     * predefined whitelist are accepted.
     *
     * @param array $preferences
     * @return array
     */
    private function validateAndSanitize(array $preferences): array
    {
        $sanitized = [];

        foreach (self::ALLOWED_KEYS as $key => $expectedType) {
            if (!array_key_exists($key, $preferences)) {
                // Use default if key is missing
                $sanitized[$key] = self::DEFAULTS[$key];
                continue;
            }

            $value = $preferences[$key];

            // Validate type matches expected type
            if (!$this->isValidType($value, $expectedType)) {
                Log::warning("UserPreferencesHandler: Invalid type for preference '{$key}'.", [
                    'expected' => $expectedType,
                    'actual' => gettype($value),
                ]);
                $sanitized[$key] = self::DEFAULTS[$key];
                continue;
            }

            // Additional value-specific validation
            $validatedValue = $this->validateValue($key, $value);
            if ($validatedValue === null) {
                Log::warning("UserPreferencesHandler: Invalid value for preference '{$key}'.");
                $sanitized[$key] = self::DEFAULTS[$key];
                continue;
            }

            $sanitized[$key] = $validatedValue;
        }

        return $sanitized;
    }

    /**
     * Check if a value matches the expected type.
     *
     * @param mixed $value
     * @param string $expectedType
     * @return bool
     */
    private function isValidType(mixed $value, string $expectedType): bool
    {
        return match ($expectedType) {
            'string' => is_string($value),
            'integer' => is_int($value),
            'boolean' => is_bool($value),
            'float' => is_float($value) || is_int($value),
            default => false,
        };
    }

    /**
     * Validate specific preference values against allowed ranges/options.
     *
     * @param string $key
     * @param mixed $value
     * @return mixed|null Returns null if validation fails.
     */
    private function validateValue(string $key, mixed $value): mixed
    {
        return match ($key) {
            'theme' => in_array($value, ['light', 'dark', 'auto'], true) ? $value : null,
            'language' => preg_match('/^[a-z]{2}(-[A-Z]{2})?$/', $value) ? $value : null,
            'timezone' => in_array($value, timezone_identifiers_list(), true) ? $value : null,
            'font_size' => ($value >= 8 && $value <= 32) ? $value : null,
            'notifications_enabled' => $value, // already validated as boolean
            'sidebar_collapsed' => $value, // already validated as boolean
            'items_per_page' => in_array($value, [10, 25, 50, 100], true) ? $value : null,
            'date_format' => in_array($value, ['Y-m-d', 'd/m/Y', 'm/d/Y', 'd.m.Y'], true) ? $value : null,
            'currency' => preg_match('/^[A-Z]{3}$/', $value) ? $value : null,
            'layout' => in_array($value, ['default', 'compact', 'wide'], true) ? $value : null,
            default => null,
        };
    }
}
